raw_text:
Principal Security Engineer
SecureVault Systems — Zero-Trust Infrastructure for Enterprise Cloud

Location

San Francisco, CA (Remote-Flexible)

Employment Type

Full-Time

Compensation

$280,000 - $380,000 USD annually

The Opportunity

SecureVault Systems is seeking a Principal Security Engineer to lead organization-wide security architecture
strategy and define technical direction for our AppSec, threat detection, and cloud security initiatives. In this

strategic role, you will architect zero-trust frameworks, establish security standards across engineering teams,
and oversee the technical roadmap for our SIEM and threat modeling capabilities. This is a unique opportunity

to shape security practices at scale and mentor security architects across the organization.

About SecureVault Systems

SecureVault Systems is a rapidly growing SaaS-based security infrastructure company dedicated to helping
enterprises implement zero-trust architecture and advanced threat detection across multi-cloud environments.

Founded in 2018, we serve Fortune 500 companies and mid-market organizations, providing unified security
orchestration and real-time threat intelligence platforms.

Day-to-Day

Define and communicate org-wide security architecture standards, threat modeling methodologies, and

AppSec frameworks (OWASP Top 10, secure coding practices) across all engineering squads

Lead technical strategy for SIEM platform evolution, designing advanced threat detection rules and analytics
pipelines that scale across petabyte-scale log ingestion

Architect and oversee cloud security posture management systems, including AWS IAM privilege escalation
detection, network segmentation policies, and automated compliance verification

Conduct sophisticated threat modeling exercises for critical systems, establishing risk matrices and security
controls aligned with organizational risk tolerance

Build and mentor a team of 4-6 senior security engineers and architects, reviewing designs, establishing

hiring standards, and driving technical growth

Drive penetration testing strategy and red team operations, defining scope, security baselines, and

remediation prioritization across the engineering organization

Lead security toolchain evaluation and buildout, including SAST/DAST tool integration, secrets
management, vulnerability management, and forensic investigation capabilities

Establish Python-based automation frameworks for security testing, policy enforcement, and incident
response workflows across infrastructure and applications

Present quarterly security posture reviews to C-suite and board, translating technical risk into business impact
and resource recommendations

 You Have

12+ years of hands-on security engineering experience, with at least 5 years in a staff-level or senior architect
role defining organization-wide security strategy

Deep expertise in Application Security (AppSec), including OWASP frameworks, secure code review, threat

modeling (STRIDE/PASTA), and vulnerability assessment methodologies

Advanced proficiency with SIEM platforms (Splunk, ELK, Datadog Log Management) including complex

query optimization, alert tuning, and rule development at enterprise scale

Expert-level AWS security knowledge including IAM policies, KMS, Security Groups, VPC design,
CloudTrail/GuardDuty integration, and compliance automation (Config)

Demonstrated expertise in penetration testing methodologies, red teaming, and advanced attack simulation;

familiarity with frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK

Strong Python proficiency for security automation, with proven ability to build scalable security tools,
integrations, and monitoring scripts

Track record of architecting and implementing zero-trust security models in production multi-cloud
environments

Bonus

CISSP, CCSK, or equivalent advanced security certification demonstrating breadth across security domains

Published research, conference talks, or open-source contributions in application security, threat detection, or
cloud security

Experience leading security transformations or building security functions from the ground up in high-growth
companies

Hands-on experience with threat modeling tools (Microsoft Threat Modeling Tool, IriusRisk) and secure
architecture frameworks

Knowledge of container security, Kubernetes RBAC, supply chain security, and software bill of materials
(SBOM) practices

Our Stack

OWASP (Top 10, SAMM, Threat Modeling)   Splunk/ELK Stack (SIEM)   AWS IAM & GuardDuty

Threat Modeling (STRIDE/PASTA frameworks)   Python (security automation, scripting)

Penetration Testing Tools (Burp Suite, Metasploit, custom frameworks)

AppSec SAST/DAST Tools (SonarQube, Veracode, Snyk)   Container Security (Docker, Kubernetes, Falco)

Secrets Management (HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager)

Incident Response & Forensics Tools (Velociraptor, osquery, Wireshark)

What We Offer

Competitive equity package (0.25%-0.5% depending on background) with standard 4-year vesting

Comprehensive health coverage including medical, dental, vision with company covering 95% of employee
premiums and 80% of dependent premiums

$15,000 annual professional development budget for conferences, certifications (CISSP, CCSK, etc.),
training, and security research tools

 Flexible work-from-home policy with optional access to San Francisco Bay Area office; $2,000 home office
stipend

Unlimited PTO with a minimum 5-week annual recommendation; 10 company holidays plus 1 volunteer day
per quarter

401(k) with 5% company match, fully vested immediately; ESPP with 15% discount

Parental leave (16 weeks primary, 12 weeks secondary); backup childcare and fertility benefits

Mental health support via Headspace subscription and quarterly wellness workshops; free on-site fitness

center access

Apply

Submit your resume, a cover letter describing your most significant security architecture accomplishment, and
links to any publications/talks or GitHub security projects to careers@securevault-systems.com with the subject
line 'Principal Security Engineer - [Your Name]'. Candidates will be contacted within 5 business days for initial
screening conversations. We also accept referrals through our internal employee referral program with a $10,000
bonus upon hire completion.
